---
name: Submit a source
about: Suggest a source (article, report, filing, study) for use in claim research.
title: "[source] "
labels: source-submission
---

## Source URL

<!-- Required. The canonical URL of the source. -->

## What does this source say?

<!-- One or two sentences. What does the source assert that is relevant to an AI company,
product, or sector covered on dangerousrobot.org? -->

## Which entity does it relate to?

<!-- Company, product, or sector. Use the slug if you know it (e.g., `companies/anthropic`,
`products/claude`, `sectors/ai-llm-producers`). If unsure, name the organization. -->

## Which existing claim does it inform, if any?

<!-- Optional. Paste a claim URL or slug if this source supports, contradicts, or updates
an existing verdict. Otherwise leave blank — the source may seed a new claim. -->

## Source kind

<!-- Pick one. -->

- [ ] Primary (organization's own output: disclosure, official documentation, filing)
- [ ] Secondary (independent reporting, academic paper, third-party analysis)
- [ ] Tertiary (advocacy, opinion, consumer guide, aggregator)

## Anything else worth flagging?

<!-- Date sensitivity, paywall status, archive availability, conflict-of-interest concerns,
or other context the maintainer should know before ingesting. -->

---

By submitting, you confirm the source is publicly accessible (or note paywall/archive
status above) and that you have the right to point to it. Submitted sources are reviewed
before ingestion; not every submission becomes a citation.
